assertions.description: Direct Sockets is gated to Isolated Web Apps. In a normal web context the UDPSocket constructor is not exposed.
TCPSocket is similarly gated; multicast is an extension on top of the same IWA-only surface.
Direct Sockets requires a secure context; IWAs are inherently secure.
UDPSocket exposes readable/writable as streams; ReadableStream is available so userland can consume incoming multicast datagrams.
UDPSocket's writable side is a WritableStream; the constructor is available for the multicast send path.
Attempting to construct UDPSocket outside an Isolated Web App throws — the gating is at construction time.
